EyeDropper is a Chromium-only browser API with no `PlatformEffects` method. How should the agnostic core expose this?

options.label: Adapter-sent event
options.description: Add `Event::SetEyedropperSupported(bool)`; emit a typed `Effect::DetectEyedropper` on open; the adapter performs `'EyeDropper' in window` detection and sends the event back. Keeps detection in the adapter (where window lives) and core agnostic. Add the event to spec §1.2.

options.label: Prop
options.description: Make `eyedropper_supported` a `Props` field the adapter sets, synced into Context via SetProps. Simpler, but conflates a runtime browser capability with configuration.

questions.question: For the 2D area and channel-slider drag inside the picker, how should pointer tracking be wired?

options.label: Adapter-driven (match siblings)
options.description: No machine drag effect: adapter attaches pointer listeners on pointerdown and drives DragStart/DragMove/DragEnd, exactly like color_area/color_slider. State stays Dragging{target}. Consistent with the 7 siblings; drops the spec's drag-listeners effect.

options.label: Typed AttachPointerDrag effect
options.description: Emit `Effect::AttachPointerDrag` on DragStart so the adapter wires `platform.track_pointer_drag`. More popover-like, but inconsistent with how the sibling area/slider already handle dragging.

answers.For the 2D area and channel-slider drag: Take the approach that will yield the best outcome possible to our library, to make it more useful

content.text: The drag-wiring answer ("best outcome / most useful") combined with the issue's note that area/slider measurement is an adapter concern points to the adapter-driven approach: the adapter measures the element and sends already-normalized coords, which avoids needing element rects inside effect closures and stays consistent with all 7 siblings. Let me confirm one detail — how `EffectName` is satisfied for a derived enum — then write the plan.

input.command:
echo "=== EffectName trait + impls ==="; grep -n "EffectName" crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs | head; echo "---"; sed -n '/trait EffectName/,/^}/p' crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs | head -30; echo "--- blanket impl? ---"; grep -n "impl.*EffectName.*for\|impl<.*> EffectName" crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs
echo; echo "=== color_area Effect derive line ==="; grep -n "impl ars_core::EffectName\|EffectName" crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_area/mod.rs
echo; echo "=== ComponentIds::item / item_part sigs ==="; sed -n '/pub fn item\b/,/}/p;/pub fn item_part/,/}/p;/pub fn part\b/,/}/p' crates/ars-core/src/component_ids.rs | head -40
input.description: Verify EffectName impl and ComponentIds methods

content.text: I have everything needed. One important constraint surfaced: `ComponentPart` requires `Eq + Hash`, but the spec's `Part::Swatch { index, color: ColorValue }` can't derive those (`ColorValue` holds `f64`). I'll resolve this by giving the picker a `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>` preset prop (matching `color_swatch_picker`'s `colors` pattern) so `Part::Swatch { index }` resolves its color from context. Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# Task #300 — Implement ColorPicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #300 (epic #227) asks for the framework-agnostic core of the **ColorPicker**
complex orchestrator: composite color state, popover open/close lifecycle, a 2D
saturation/lightness area + hue/alpha channel sliders, channel text inputs, hex input,
format switching (hex/rgb/hsl/hsb), color-space switching, swatch presets, EyeDropper
integration, form `HiddenInput`, and full ARIA/`data-ars-*` output. Adapters (Leptos/Dioxus)
are explicitly **out of scope**. Dependencies #297/#298/#299 are merged — the shared
`ColorValue`/`ColorFormat`/`ColorSpace`/`ColorChannel`/`DragTarget` types and the
`channel_value`/`with_channel`/`channel_range`/`format_color_string`/`parse_color_string`
helpers already live in `crates/ars-core/src/color.rs` and are re-exported at the crate root.

**Why the spec needs synchronizing in this PR.** The color-picker spec §1 (State Machine /
Connect) was authored in an early prototype style that predates the 7 sibling color
components shipped in #297–#299. It uses string-named effects and undefined helpers
(`add_click_outside_listener_multi`, `is_eyedropper_available`, `invoke_eyedropper`,
`use_platform_effects()` inside core), precomputes ~13 id-`String` Context fields and an
`id: ComponentId` (no `ComponentId` type exists — only `ComponentIds`), and omits the
now-required typed `Machine::Effect` associated type. It cannot compile against the current
`Machine` trait. Per the CLAUDE.md spec-sync rule, the implementation follows the canonical
sibling conventions and the spec §1 is rewritten to match in the same PR (user-confirmed:
"Align + update spec §1"). Two confirmed design decisions: eyedropper support is set via an
adapter-sent `Event::SetEyedropperSupported(bool)` (the spec's detect effect had no setter);
drag is adapter-driven (adapter measures the element and sends normalized `DragMove`/`DragEnd`,
matching all 7 siblings and avoiding element measurement inside core effect closures).

## Canonical conventions to follow (from `color_area`/`color_slider`/`popover`)

- `Bindable<T>` for controlled/uncontrolled `value` and `open`; `.get()`/`.set()`/`.pending()`/`.sync_controlled()`.
- Typed `Effect` enum (`#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]`); `EffectName` is blanket-impl'd. Effects emitted via `PendingEffect::named(...)` / `PendingEffect::new(Effect::X, |ctx, props, send| ... )`; adapters wire the actual DOM behavior.
- `Context` stores `ids: ComponentIds` (built once via `ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id)`); part ids derived with `ids.part("trigger")`, channel inputs with `ids.item("channel", &index)`.
- `on_props_changed(old, new) -> Vec<Event>`: assert id stable; controlled `open` change → `Open`/`Close` (popover pattern); controlled `value` change → `SyncValue`; other context-relevant props → `SetProps`.
- `initial_effects(state, ctx, props)`: when `state == Open` (booted open), mirror the open-lifecycle effects so adapters drive the same wiring on first mount via `Service::take_initial_effects()`.
- Tests: `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(), &Messages::default())`; snapshots via `insta::assert_snapshot!` of `format!("{attrs:#?}")`.

## Files

- **New:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_picker/mod.rs` (machine + connect + unit/snapshot tests).
- **New:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_picker/snapshots/*.snap` (committed insta fixtures).
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs` — add `pub mod color_picker;` with a `///` doc line.
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/specialized.rs` — add `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` (all 17 parts).
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs` — add a `color_picker_proptests` module (arbitrary event sequence keeps value finite/in-range and state/open consistent).
- **Edit:** `spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md` — sync §1.2/1.3/1.4/1.5/1.7/1.8 (+ §2 if Part text shifts) to the implemented contract.

## Implementation (`color_picker/mod.rs`)

Reuse from `ars_core`: `ColorValue, ColorFormat, ColorSpace, ColorChannel, DragTarget, channel_value, with_channel, channel_range, format_color_string`. Import `Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, MessageFn, PendingEffect, TransitionPlan, no_cleanup, AttrMap, HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, CssProperty, Direction, Locale, Env`, `ars_dom::positioning::{PositioningOptions, Placement, Offset}`, `ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData`/`KeyboardKey`.

- **State** (§1.1): `Closed`, `Open`, `Dragging { target: DragTarget }`. `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]`.
- **Event** (§1.2): the spec events — `Open, Close, Toggle, DragStart{target,x,y}, DragMove{x,y}, DragEnd, SetColor(ColorValue), SetChannel{channel,value}, SetFormat(ColorFormat), ChangeColorSpace(ColorSpace), EyedropperRequest, EyedropperResult(Option<ColorValue>), Focus{part:&'static str}, Blur{part:&'static str}, ChannelIncrement{channel,step}, ChannelDecrement{channel,step}, CloseOnInteractOutside, CloseOnEscape` — **plus** `SetEyedropperSupported(bool)`, `SyncValue(Option<ColorValue>)`, `SetProps`. All `Copy`.
- **Effect**: `ChangeEnd, AttachClickOutside, DetachClickOutside, DetectEyedropper, InvokeEyedropper, AnnounceColorSpace`.
- **Context** (§1.3 revised): `value: Bindable<ColorValue>`, `open: Bindable<bool>`, `format: ColorFormat`, `disabled/readonly/close_on_interact_outside/close_on_escape/show_alpha: bool`, `eyedropper_supported: bool`, `focused_part: Option<&'static str>`, `positioning: PositioningOptions`, `channel_step/channel_large_step: f64`, `color_space: ColorSpace`, `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>`, `dir: Direction`, `locale: Locale`, `messages: Messages`, `ids: ComponentIds`. Method `channels(&self) -> &[ColorChannel]` per `color_space`.
- **Props** (§1.4 revised): all spec props (`value, default_value, open, default_open, disabled, readonly, close_on_interact_outside, close_on_escape, show_alpha, default_format, positioning, channel_step, channel_large_step, color_space, dir, name, id`) + `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>` + `on_change_end: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(ColorValue)+Send+Sync>>`. `Default` matches spec (positioning `BottomStart`, offset main 4.0; steps 1.0/10.0; show_alpha true; close_on_* true).
- **Messages** (§4.1): all 14 `MessageFn` fields with the spec's `Default` impl, verbatim. `impl ComponentMessages`.
- **`apply_drag_position(ctx, target, x, y)`** (§1.7): `Area` → `sat=x.clamp`, `light=(1-y).clamp`, preserve hue/alpha; `Channel(ch)` → `min + x.clamp*(max-min)` via `with_channel`. Base on `*ctx.value.pending()` so controlled drags accumulate.
- **`change_end_effect()`**: `PendingEffect::new(Effect::ChangeEnd, ...)` invoking `props.on_change_end` with `*ctx.value.pending()`.
- **Machine::transition**: disabled guard returns `None` for all but `Focus`/`Blur`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`. Arms per spec mapped to typed effects:
  - `Closed + (Open|Toggle)` → `to(Open)`, `open.set(true)`, effects `AttachClickOutside` + `DetectEyedropper`.
  - `Open + (Close|Toggle)` → `to(Closed)`, `open.set(false)`, effect `DetachClickOutside`.
  - `Open + CloseOnInteractOutside` (guard `close_on_interact_outside`) / `Open + CloseOnEscape` (guard `close_on_escape`) → close. `Dragging + CloseOnInteractOutside` → `None` (suppress during drag).
  - `Open + DragStart` (guard `!readonly`) → `to(Dragging{target})` + `apply_drag_position`.
  - `Dragging{target} + DragMove` (guard `!readonly`) → `context_only` apply. `Dragging + DragEnd` → `to(Open)` + `change_end_effect`.
  - `(any) + SetColor` (guard `!readonly`) → set value. `Open + SetChannel` (guard `!readonly`) → `with_channel`. `(any) + SetFormat` → set format.
  - `(any) + ChangeColorSpace` → set `color_space` + effect `AnnounceColorSpace` (closure uses `ctx.messages.color_space_switched(&format!("{new_space:?}"), &ctx.locale)`; the **announce call itself is adapter-side** — core just emits the named effect, no `use_platform_effects` in core).
  - `Open + ChannelIncrement/Decrement` (guard `!readonly`) → clamp to `channel_range`.
  - `Open + EyedropperRequest` (guard `eyedropper_supported && !readonly`) → effect `InvokeEyedropper`. `(any) + EyedropperResult(Some)` → set value; `None` → `None`.
  - `(any) + SetEyedropperSupported(b)` → set flag. `(any) + Focus/Blur` → set/clear `focused_part`.
  - `(any) + SyncValue(v)` → `sync_controlled`. `(any) + SetProps` → sync mutable context props from `props`.
- **on_props_changed / initial_effects**: as in Canonical conventions above.
- **Part** (§1.8 revised): `Root, Label, Control, Trigger, Content, Area, AreaThumb, ChannelSlider{channel}, ChannelSliderThumb{channel}, AlphaSlider, SwatchGroup, Swatch{index}, FormatSelect, ChannelInput{channel,index}, HexInput, EyeDropperTrigger, HiddenInput`. `#[scope="color-picker"]`. **`Swatch` carries only `index`** (not `color: ColorValue` — `ColorValue` is not `Eq`/`Hash`, which `ComponentPart` requires); the color resolves from `ctx.swatches[index]`, matching `color_swatch_picker::Part::Item`.
- **Api** (§1.8): all `*_attrs` methods (ids via `ctx.ids.part(...)`); computed `is_open/is_dragging/value/value_as_string/format/color_name/color_announcement`; imperative `open/close/set_value/set_format`; typed dispatch helpers (`on_trigger_click`, `on_content_keydown` for Escape, `on_area_pointer_down`, `on_channel_slider_keydown`, `on_swatch_click`, `on_eyedropper_click`). `swatch_attrs(index)` reads `ctx.swatches.get(index)`. `ConnectApi::part_attrs` exhaustive over `Part`. Use `HtmlAttr::Role` (there is no `AriaAttr::Role`).

## Tests (TDD — write failing tests first)

**Unit (in-module `#[cfg(test)]`)** covering every issue bullet + acceptance: trigger `aria-haspopup="dialog"`/`aria-expanded`/`aria-controls`/`aria-labelledby`; open/close/toggle + `data-ars-state`; area drag updates sat+light; channel-slider drag updates channel; `channels()` per color space (RGB vs HSL); alpha slider attrs gated by `show_alpha`; `SetFormat` changes `value_as_string` across hex/rgb/hsl/hsb; eyedropper gating (`SetEyedropperSupported` enables `EyedropperRequest`; trigger `hidden` when unsupported; `EyedropperResult(Some)` sets value); swatch selected/`data-ars-index`; content `role="dialog"`+`aria-labelledby`; hidden input `name`+hex `value`; disabled/readonly guards; channel increment/decrement clamping; `ChangeColorSpace` emits `AnnounceColorSpace` + updates `color_space`; `on_change_end` fires on `DragEnd` with pending value; controlled open+value sync via `set_props`; `Dragging` suppresses `CloseOnInteractOutside`; `initial_effects` when `default_open`; one exhaustive events/parts walk for llvm-cov.

**Snapshot (committed `.snap`)** — one per anatomy part and each output-affecting branch, satisfying the acceptance criterion and `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`: root (closed/open/disabled/readonly), label, control, trigger (closed/open/disabled), content (open), area, area_thumb (idle/dragging), channel_slider+thumb (hue, dragging), alpha_slider, swatch_group, swatch (selected/unselected), format_select, channel_input (rgb), hex_input, eye_dropper_trigger (supported/unsupported), hidden_input.

**Spec-conformance:** `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 17 `(Part, kebab-name)` pairs.

**Proptest:** `color_picker_proptests` — arbitrary `Event` sequence; assert value stays finite + channels in range and `is_open`/state stay consistent; reuse `super::common::proptest_config()`.

## Spec sync (`spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md`)

Rewrite §1.2 (add `SetEyedropperSupported`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`), §1.3 (Context: `ids: ComponentIds` + `swatches`, drop precomputed id-String fields + `id: ComponentId`), §1.4 (Props: add `swatches`; `on_change_end` `Callback` type), §1.5 (`is_open` reads `ctx.open.get()`), §1.7 (typed `Effect` enum; `initial_effects`/`on_props_changed`; `apply_drag_position` on `pending()`; remove undefined helpers; note drag is adapter-driven and announce/click-outside/eyedropper are adapter-wired named effects), §1.8 (`Part::Swatch{index}`, `swatch_attrs(index)`). Keep §2 anatomy table (Swatch row stays; note color resolves from `swatches`).

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p ars-components specialized::color_picker` (unit + snapshots) — green, zero warnings.
2. `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` then `cargo insta accept` once fixtures are reviewed; commit `.snap` files; apply the `snapshot-reviewed` PR label.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance specialized::color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec`.
4. `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines specialized::color_picker_proptests`.
5. `cargo xtask spec validate` (frontmatter ↔ manifest) after spec edits.
6. Run the **`post-implementation-audit`** skill (mandatory): spec/impl drift, iterative "anything missing?", and test-coverage audit; land every finding in this PR.
7. After user approval: `cargo xci-fast`, fix failures, then commit + push a PR to `main` with `Closes #300`, add `snapshot-reviewed` label, and run the **`waiting-for-codex-review`** skill.

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/ericson/.claude/plans/implement-task-300-wise-wozniak.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

**Spec-conformance:** `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 17 `(Part, kebab-name)` pairs.

**Proptest:** `color_picker_proptests` — arbitrary `Event` sequence; assert value stays finite + channels in range and `is_open`/state stay consistent; reuse `super::common::proptest_config()`.

## Spec sync (`spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md`)

Rewrite §1.2 (add `SetEyedropperSupported`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`), §1.3 (Context: `ids: ComponentIds` + `swatches`, drop precomputed id-String fields + `id: ComponentId`), §1.4 (Props: add `swatches`; `on_change_end` `Callback` type), §1.5 (`is_open` reads `ctx.open.get()`), §1.7 (typed `Effect` enum; `initial_effects`/`on_props_changed`; `apply_drag_position` on `pending()`; remove undefined helpers; note drag is adapter-driven and announce/click-outside/eyedropper are adapter-wired named effects), §1.8 (`Part::Swatch{index}`, `swatch_attrs(index)`). Keep §2 anatomy table (Swatch row stays; note color resolves from `swatches`).

# Task #300 — Implement ColorPicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #300 (epic #227) asks for the framework-agnostic core of the **ColorPicker**
complex orchestrator: composite color state, popover open/close lifecycle, a 2D
saturation/lightness area + hue/alpha channel sliders, channel text inputs, hex input,
format switching (hex/rgb/hsl/hsb), color-space switching, swatch presets, EyeDropper
integration, form `HiddenInput`, and full ARIA/`data-ars-*` output. Adapters (Leptos/Dioxus)
are explicitly **out of scope**. Dependencies #297/#298/#299 are merged — the shared
`ColorValue`/`ColorFormat`/`ColorSpace`/`ColorChannel`/`DragTarget` types and the
`channel_value`/`with_channel`/`channel_range`/`format_color_string`/`parse_color_string`
helpers already live in `crates/ars-core/src/color.rs` and are re-exported at the crate root.

**Why the spec needs synchronizing in this PR.** The color-picker spec §1 (State Machine /
Connect) was authored in an early prototype style that predates the 7 sibling color
components shipped in #297–#299. It uses string-named effects and undefined helpers
(`add_click_outside_listener_multi`, `is_eyedropper_available`, `invoke_eyedropper`,
`use_platform_effects()` inside core), precomputes ~13 id-`String` Context fields and an
`id: ComponentId` (no `ComponentId` type exists — only `ComponentIds`), and omits the
now-required typed `Machine::Effect` associated type. It cannot compile against the current
`Machine` trait. Per the CLAUDE.md spec-sync rule, the implementation follows the canonical
sibling conventions and the spec §1 is rewritten to match in the same PR (user-confirmed:
"Align + update spec §1"). Two confirmed design decisions: eyedropper support is set via an
adapter-sent `Event::SetEyedropperSupported(bool)` (the spec's detect effect had no setter);
drag is adapter-driven (adapter measures the element and sends normalized `DragMove`/`DragEnd`,
matching all 7 siblings and avoiding element measurement inside core effect closures).

## Canonical conventions to follow (from `color_area`/`color_slider`/`popover`)

- `Bindable<T>` for controlled/uncontrolled `value` and `open`; `.get()`/`.set()`/`.pending()`/`.sync_controlled()`.
- Typed `Effect` enum (`#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]`); `EffectName` is blanket-impl'd. Effects emitted via `PendingEffect::named(...)` / `PendingEffect::new(Effect::X, |ctx, props, send| ... )`; adapters wire the actual DOM behavior.
- `Context` stores `ids: ComponentIds` (built once via `ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id)`); part ids derived with `ids.part("trigger")`, channel inputs with `ids.item("channel", &index)`.
- `on_props_changed(old, new) -> Vec<Event>`: assert id stable; controlled `open` change → `Open`/`Close` (popover pattern); controlled `value` change → `SyncValue`; other context-relevant props → `SetProps`.
- `initial_effects(state, ctx, props)`: when `state == Open` (booted open), mirror the open-lifecycle effects so adapters drive the same wiring on first mount via `Service::take_initial_effects()`.
- Tests: `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(), &Messages::default())`; snapshots via `insta::assert_snapshot!` of `format!("{attrs:#?}")`.

## Files

- **New:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_picker/mod.rs` (machine + connect + unit/snapshot tests).
- **New:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_picker/snapshots/*.snap` (committed insta fixtures).
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs` — add `pub mod color_picker;` with a `///` doc line.
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/specialized.rs` — add `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` (all 17 parts).
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs` — add a `color_picker_proptests` module (arbitrary event sequence keeps value finite/in-range and state/open consistent).
- **Edit:** `spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md` — sync §1.2/1.3/1.4/1.5/1.7/1.8 (+ §2 if Part text shifts) to the implemented contract.

## Implementation (`color_picker/mod.rs`)

Reuse from `ars_core`: `ColorValue, ColorFormat, ColorSpace, ColorChannel, DragTarget, channel_value, with_channel, channel_range, format_color_string`. Import `Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, MessageFn, PendingEffect, TransitionPlan, no_cleanup, AttrMap, HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, CssProperty, Direction, Locale, Env`, `ars_dom::positioning::{PositioningOptions, Placement, Offset}`, `ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData`/`KeyboardKey`.

- **State** (§1.1): `Closed`, `Open`, `Dragging { target: DragTarget }`. `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]`.
- **Event** (§1.2): the spec events — `Open, Close, Toggle, DragStart{target,x,y}, DragMove{x,y}, DragEnd, SetColor(ColorValue), SetChannel{channel,value}, SetFormat(ColorFormat), ChangeColorSpace(ColorSpace), EyedropperRequest, EyedropperResult(Option<ColorValue>), Focus{part:&'static str}, Blur{part:&'static str}, ChannelIncrement{channel,step}, ChannelDecrement{channel,step}, CloseOnInteractOutside, CloseOnEscape` — **plus** `SetEyedropperSupported(bool)`, `SyncValue(Option<ColorValue>)`, `SetProps`. All `Copy`.
- **Effect**: `ChangeEnd, AttachClickOutside, DetachClickOutside, DetectEyedropper, InvokeEyedropper, AnnounceColorSpace`.
- **Context** (§1.3 revised): `value: Bindable<ColorValue>`, `open: Bindable<bool>`, `format: ColorFormat`, `disabled/readonly/close_on_interact_outside/close_on_escape/show_alpha: bool`, `eyedropper_supported: bool`, `focused_part: Option<&'static str>`, `positioning: PositioningOptions`, `channel_step/channel_large_step: f64`, `color_space: ColorSpace`, `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>`, `dir: Direction`, `locale: Locale`, `messages: Messages`, `ids: ComponentIds`. Method `channels(&self) -> &[ColorChannel]` per `color_space`.
- **Props** (§1.4 revised): all spec props (`value, default_value, open, default_open, disabled, readonly, close_on_interact_outside, close_on_escape, show_alpha, default_format, positioning, channel_step, channel_large_step, color_space, dir, name, id`) + `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>` + `on_change_end: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(ColorValue)+Send+Sync>>`. `Default` matches spec (positioning `BottomStart`, offset main 4.0; steps 1.0/10.0; show_alpha true; close_on_* true).
- **Messages** (§4.1): all 14 `MessageFn` fields with the spec's `Default` impl, verbatim. `impl ComponentMessages`.
- **`apply_drag_position(ctx, target, x, y)`** (§1.7): `Area` → `sat=x.clamp`, `light=(1-y).clamp`, preserve hue/alpha; `Channel(ch)` → `min + x.clamp*(max-min)` via `with_channel`. Base on `*ctx.value.pending()` so controlled drags accumulate.
- **`change_end_effect()`**: `PendingEffect::new(Effect::ChangeEnd, ...)` invoking `props.on_change_end` with `*ctx.value.pending()`.
- **Machine::transition**: disabled guard returns `None` for all but `Focus`/`Blur`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`. Arms per spec mapped to typed effects:
  - `Closed + (Open|Toggle)` → `to(Open)`, `open.set(true)`, effects `AttachClickOutside` + `DetectEyedropper`.
  - `Open + (Close|Toggle)` → `to(Closed)`, `open.set(false)`, effect `DetachClickOutside`.
  - `Open + CloseOnInteractOutside` (guard `close_on_interact_outside`) / `Open + CloseOnEscape` (guard `close_on_escape`) → close. `Dragging + CloseOnInteractOutside` → `None` (suppress during drag).
  - `Open + DragStart` (guard `!readonly`) → `to(Dragging{target})` + `apply_drag_position`.
  - `Dragging{target} + DragMove` (guard `!readonly`) → `context_only` apply. `Dragging + DragEnd` → `to(Open)` + `change_end_effect`.
  - `(any) + SetColor` (guard `!readonly`) → set value. `Open + SetChannel` (guard `!readonly`) → `with_channel`. `(any) + SetFormat` → set format.
  - `(any) + ChangeColorSpace` → set `color_space` + effect `AnnounceColorSpace` (closure uses `ctx.messages.color_space_switched(&format!("{new_space:?}"), &ctx.locale)`; the **announce call itself is adapter-side** — core just emits the named effect, no `use_platform_effects` in core).
  - `Open + ChannelIncrement/Decrement` (guard `!readonly`) → clamp to `channel_range`.
  - `Open + EyedropperRequest` (guard `eyedropper_supported && !readonly`) → effect `InvokeEyedropper`. `(any) + EyedropperResult(Some)` → set value; `None` → `None`.
  - `(any) + SetEyedropperSupported(b)` → set flag. `(any) + Focus/Blur` → set/clear `focused_part`.
  - `(any) + SyncValue(v)` → `sync_controlled`. `(any) + SetProps` → sync mutable context props from `props`.
- **on_props_changed / initial_effects**: as in Canonical conventions above.
- **Part** (§1.8 revised): `Root, Label, Control, Trigger, Content, Area, AreaThumb, ChannelSlider{channel}, ChannelSliderThumb{channel}, AlphaSlider, SwatchGroup, Swatch{index}, FormatSelect, ChannelInput{channel,index}, HexInput, EyeDropperTrigger, HiddenInput`. `#[scope="color-picker"]`. **`Swatch` carries only `index`** (not `color: ColorValue` — `ColorValue` is not `Eq`/`Hash`, which `ComponentPart` requires); the color resolves from `ctx.swatches[index]`, matching `color_swatch_picker::Part::Item`.
- **Api** (§1.8): all `*_attrs` methods (ids via `ctx.ids.part(...)`); computed `is_open/is_dragging/value/value_as_string/format/color_name/color_announcement`; imperative `open/close/set_value/set_format`; typed dispatch helpers (`on_trigger_click`, `on_content_keydown` for Escape, `on_area_pointer_down`, `on_channel_slider_keydown`, `on_swatch_click`, `on_eyedropper_click`). `swatch_attrs(index)` reads `ctx.swatches.get(index)`. `ConnectApi::part_attrs` exhaustive over `Part`. Use `HtmlAttr::Role` (there is no `AriaAttr::Role`).

## Tests (TDD — write failing tests first)

**Unit (in-module `#[cfg(test)]`)** covering every issue bullet + acceptance: trigger `aria-haspopup="dialog"`/`aria-expanded`/`aria-controls`/`aria-labelledby`; open/close/toggle + `data-ars-state`; area drag updates sat+light; channel-slider drag updates channel; `channels()` per color space (RGB vs HSL); alpha slider attrs gated by `show_alpha`; `SetFormat` changes `value_as_string` across hex/rgb/hsl/hsb; eyedropper gating (`SetEyedropperSupported` enables `EyedropperRequest`; trigger `hidden` when unsupported; `EyedropperResult(Some)` sets value); swatch selected/`data-ars-index`; content `role="dialog"`+`aria-labelledby`; hidden input `name`+hex `value`; disabled/readonly guards; channel increment/decrement clamping; `ChangeColorSpace` emits `AnnounceColorSpace` + updates `color_space`; `on_change_end` fires on `DragEnd` with pending value; controlled open+value sync via `set_props`; `Dragging` suppresses `CloseOnInteractOutside`; `initial_effects` when `default_open`; one exhaustive events/parts walk for llvm-cov.

**Snapshot (committed `.snap`)** — one per anatomy part and each output-affecting branch, satisfying the acceptance criterion and `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`: root (closed/open/disabled/readonly), label, control, trigger (closed/open/disabled), content (open), area, area_thumb (idle/dragging), channel_slider+thumb (hue, dragging), alpha_slider, swatch_group, swatch (selected/unselected), format_select, channel_input (rgb), hex_input, eye_dropper_trigger (supported/unsupported), hidden_input.

**Spec-conformance:** `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 17 `(Part, kebab-name)` pairs.

**Proptest:** `color_picker_proptests` — arbitrary `Event` sequence; assert value stays finite + channels in range and `is_open`/state stay consistent; reuse `super::common::proptest_config()`.

## Spec sync (`spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md`)

Rewrite §1.2 (add `SetEyedropperSupported`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`), §1.3 (Context: `ids: ComponentIds` + `swatches`, drop precomputed id-String fields + `id: ComponentId`), §1.4 (Props: add `swatches`; `on_change_end` `Callback` type), §1.5 (`is_open` reads `ctx.open.get()`), §1.7 (typed `Effect` enum; `initial_effects`/`on_props_changed`; `apply_drag_position` on `pending()`; remove undefined helpers; note drag is adapter-driven and announce/click-outside/eyedropper are adapter-wired named effects), §1.8 (`Part::Swatch{index}`, `swatch_attrs(index)`). Keep §2 anatomy table (Swatch row stays; note color resolves from `swatches`).

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p ars-components specialized::color_picker` (unit + snapshots) — green, zero warnings.
2. `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` then `cargo insta accept` once fixtures are reviewed; commit `.snap` files; apply the `snapshot-reviewed` PR label.
3. `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance specialized::color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec`.
4. `cargo test -p ars-components --test proptest_state_machines specialized::color_picker_proptests`.
5. `cargo xtask spec validate` (frontmatter ↔ manifest) after spec edits.
6. Run the **`post-implementation-audit`** skill (mandatory): spec/impl drift, iterative "anything missing?", and test-coverage audit; land every finding in this PR.

  - `Dragging{target} + DragMove` (guard `!readonly`) → `context_only` apply. `Dragging + DragEnd` → `to(Open)` + `change_end_effect`.
  - `(any) + SetColor` (guard `!readonly`) → set value. `Open + SetChannel` (guard `!readonly`) → `with_channel`. `(any) + SetFormat` → set format.
  - `(any) + ChangeColorSpace` → set `color_space` + effect `AnnounceColorSpace` (closure uses `ctx.messages.color_space_switched(&format!("{new_space:?}"), &ctx.locale)`; the **announce call itself is adapter-side** — core just emits the named effect, no `use_platform_effects` in core).
  - `Open + ChannelIncrement/Decrement` (guard `!readonly`) → clamp to `channel_range`.
  - `Open + EyedropperRequest` (guard `eyedropper_supported && !readonly`) → effect `InvokeEyedropper`. `(any) + EyedropperResult(Some)` → set value; `None` → `None`.
  - `(any) + SetEyedropperSupported(b)` → set flag. `(any) + Focus/Blur` → set/clear `focused_part`.
  - `(any) + SyncValue(v)` → `sync_controlled`. `(any) + SetProps` → sync mutable context props from `props`.
- **on_props_changed / initial_effects**: as in Canonical conventions above.
- **Part** (§1.8 revised): `Root, Label, Control, Trigger, Content, Area, AreaThumb, ChannelSlider{channel}, ChannelSliderThumb{channel}, AlphaSlider, SwatchGroup, Swatch{index}, FormatSelect, ChannelInput{channel,index}, HexInput, EyeDropperTrigger, HiddenInput`. `#[scope="color-picker"]`. **`Swatch` carries only `index`** (not `color: ColorValue` — `ColorValue` is not `Eq`/`Hash`, which `ComponentPart` requires); the color resolves from `ctx.swatches[index]`, matching `color_swatch_picker::Part::Item`.
- **Api** (§1.8): all `*_attrs` methods (ids via `ctx.ids.part(...)`); computed `is_open/is_dragging/value/value_as_string/format/color_name/color_announcement`; imperative `open/close/set_value/set_format`; typed dispatch helpers (`on_trigger_click`, `on_content_keydown` for Escape, `on_area_pointer_down`, `on_channel_slider_keydown`, `on_swatch_click`, `on_eyedropper_click`). `swatch_attrs(index)` reads `ctx.swatches.get(index)`. `ConnectApi::part_attrs` exhaustive over `Part`. Use `HtmlAttr::Role` (there is no `AriaAttr::Role`).

## Tests (TDD — write failing tests first)

**Unit (in-module `#[cfg(test)]`)** covering every issue bullet + acceptance: trigger `aria-haspopup="dialog"`/`aria-expanded`/`aria-controls`/`aria-labelledby`; open/close/toggle + `data-ars-state`; area drag updates sat+light; channel-slider drag updates channel; `channels()` per color space (RGB vs HSL); alpha slider attrs gated by `show_alpha`; `SetFormat` changes `value_as_string` across hex/rgb/hsl/hsb; eyedropper gating (`SetEyedropperSupported` enables `EyedropperRequest`; trigger `hidden` when unsupported; `EyedropperResult(Some)` sets value); swatch selected/`data-ars-index`; content `role="dialog"`+`aria-labelledby`; hidden input `name`+hex `value`; disabled/readonly guards; channel increment/decrement clamping; `ChangeColorSpace` emits `AnnounceColorSpace` + updates `color_space`; `on_change_end` fires on `DragEnd` with pending value; controlled open+value sync via `set_props`; `Dragging` suppresses `CloseOnInteractOutside`; `initial_effects` when `default_open`; one exhaustive events/parts walk for llvm-cov.

**Snapshot (committed `.snap`)** — one per anatomy part and each output-affecting branch, satisfying the acceptance criterion and `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`: root (closed/open/disabled/readonly), label, control, trigger (closed/open/disabled), content (open), area, area_thumb (idle/dragging), channel_slider+thumb (hue, dragging), alpha_slider, swatch_group, swatch (selected/unselected), format_select, channel_input (rgb), hex_input, eye_dropper_trigger (supported/unsupported), hidden_input.

**Spec-conformance:** `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 17 `(Part, kebab-name)` pairs.

**Proptest:** `color_picker_proptests` — arbitrary `Event` sequence; assert value stays finite + channels in range and `is_open`/state stay consistent; reuse `super::common::proptest_config()`.

## Spec sync (`spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md`)

Rewrite §1.2 (add `SetEyedropperSupported`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`), §1.3 (Context: `ids: ComponentIds` + `swatches`, drop precomputed id-String fields + `id: ComponentId`), §1.4 (Props: add `swatches`; `on_change_end` `Callback` type), §1.5 (`is_open` reads `ctx.open.get()`), §1.7 (typed `Effect` enum; `initial_effects`/`on_props_changed`; `apply_drag_position` on `pending()`; remove undefined helpers; note drag is adapter-driven and announce/click-outside/eyedropper are adapter-wired named effects), §1.8 (`Part::Swatch{index}`, `swatch_attrs(index)`). Keep §2 anatomy table (Swatch row stays; note color resolves from `swatches`).

# Task #300 — Implement ColorPicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #300 (epic #227) asks for the framework-agnostic core of the **ColorPicker**
complex orchestrator: composite color state, popover open/close lifecycle, a 2D
saturation/lightness area + hue/alpha channel sliders, channel text inputs, hex input,
format switching (hex/rgb/hsl/hsb), color-space switching, swatch presets, EyeDropper
integration, form `HiddenInput`, and full ARIA/`data-ars-*` output. Adapters (Leptos/Dioxus)
are explicitly **out of scope**. Dependencies #297/#298/#299 are merged — the shared
`ColorValue`/`ColorFormat`/`ColorSpace`/`ColorChannel`/`DragTarget` types and the
`channel_value`/`with_channel`/`channel_range`/`format_color_string`/`parse_color_string`
helpers already live in `crates/ars-core/src/color.rs` and are re-exported at the crate root.

**Why the spec needs synchronizing in this PR.** The color-picker spec §1 (State Machine /
Connect) was authored in an early prototype style that predates the 7 sibling color
components shipped in #297–#299. It uses string-named effects and undefined helpers
(`add_click_outside_listener_multi`, `is_eyedropper_available`, `invoke_eyedropper`,
`use_platform_effects()` inside core), precomputes ~13 id-`String` Context fields and an
`id: ComponentId` (no `ComponentId` type exists — only `ComponentIds`), and omits the
now-required typed `Machine::Effect` associated type. It cannot compile against the current
`Machine` trait. Per the CLAUDE.md spec-sync rule, the implementation follows the canonical
sibling conventions and the spec §1 is rewritten to match in the same PR (user-confirmed:
"Align + update spec §1"). Two confirmed design decisions: eyedropper support is set via an
adapter-sent `Event::SetEyedropperSupported(bool)` (the spec's detect effect had no setter);
drag is adapter-driven (adapter measures the element and sends normalized `DragMove`/`DragEnd`,
matching all 7 siblings and avoiding element measurement inside core effect closures).

## Canonical conventions to follow (from `color_area`/`color_slider`/`popover`)

- `Bindable<T>` for controlled/uncontrolled `value` and `open`; `.get()`/`.set()`/`.pending()`/`.sync_controlled()`.
- Typed `Effect` enum (`#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]`); `EffectName` is blanket-impl'd. Effects emitted via `PendingEffect::named(...)` / `PendingEffect::new(Effect::X, |ctx, props, send| ... )`; adapters wire the actual DOM behavior.
- `Context` stores `ids: ComponentIds` (built once via `ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id)`); part ids derived with `ids.part("trigger")`, channel inputs with `ids.item("channel", &index)`.
- `on_props_changed(old, new) -> Vec<Event>`: assert id stable; controlled `open` change → `Open`/`Close` (popover pattern); controlled `value` change → `SyncValue`; other context-relevant props → `SetProps`.
- `initial_effects(state, ctx, props)`: when `state == Open` (booted open), mirror the open-lifecycle effects so adapters drive the same wiring on first mount via `Service::take_initial_effects()`.
- Tests: `Service::<Machine>::new(props, &Env::default(), &Messages::default())`; snapshots via `insta::assert_snapshot!` of `format!("{attrs:#?}")`.

## Files

- **New:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_picker/mod.rs` (machine + connect + unit/snapshot tests).
- **New:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/color_picker/snapshots/*.snap` (committed insta fixtures).
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/mod.rs` — add `pub mod color_picker;` with a `///` doc line.
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/specialized.rs` — add `color_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` (all 17 parts).
- **Edit:** `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/specialized.rs` — add a `color_picker_proptests` module (arbitrary event sequence keeps value finite/in-range and state/open consistent).
- **Edit:** `spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md` — sync §1.2/1.3/1.4/1.5/1.7/1.8 (+ §2 if Part text shifts) to the implemented contract.

## Implementation (`color_picker/mod.rs`)

Reuse from `ars_core`: `ColorValue, ColorFormat, ColorSpace, ColorChannel, DragTarget, channel_value, with_channel, channel_range, format_color_string`. Import `Bindable, Callback, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, MessageFn, PendingEffect, TransitionPlan, no_cleanup, AttrMap, HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, CssProperty, Direction, Locale, Env`, `ars_dom::positioning::{PositioningOptions, Placement, Offset}`, `ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData`/`KeyboardKey`.

- **State** (§1.1): `Closed`, `Open`, `Dragging { target: DragTarget }`. `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]`.
- **Event** (§1.2): the spec events — `Open, Close, Toggle, DragStart{target,x,y}, DragMove{x,y}, DragEnd, SetColor(ColorValue), SetChannel{channel,value}, SetFormat(ColorFormat), ChangeColorSpace(ColorSpace), EyedropperRequest, EyedropperResult(Option<ColorValue>), Focus{part:&'static str}, Blur{part:&'static str}, ChannelIncrement{channel,step}, ChannelDecrement{channel,step}, CloseOnInteractOutside, CloseOnEscape` — **plus** `SetEyedropperSupported(bool)`, `SyncValue(Option<ColorValue>)`, `SetProps`. All `Copy`.
- **Effect**: `ChangeEnd, AttachClickOutside, DetachClickOutside, DetectEyedropper, InvokeEyedropper, AnnounceColorSpace`.
- **Context** (§1.3 revised): `value: Bindable<ColorValue>`, `open: Bindable<bool>`, `format: ColorFormat`, `disabled/readonly/close_on_interact_outside/close_on_escape/show_alpha: bool`, `eyedropper_supported: bool`, `focused_part: Option<&'static str>`, `positioning: PositioningOptions`, `channel_step/channel_large_step: f64`, `color_space: ColorSpace`, `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>`, `dir: Direction`, `locale: Locale`, `messages: Messages`, `ids: ComponentIds`. Method `channels(&self) -> &[ColorChannel]` per `color_space`.
- **Props** (§1.4 revised): all spec props (`value, default_value, open, default_open, disabled, readonly, close_on_interact_outside, close_on_escape, show_alpha, default_format, positioning, channel_step, channel_large_step, color_space, dir, name, id`) + `swatches: Vec<ColorValue>` + `on_change_end: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(ColorValue)+Send+Sync>>`. `Default` matches spec (positioning `BottomStart`, offset main 4.0; steps 1.0/10.0; show_alpha true; close_on_* true).
- **Messages** (§4.1): all 14 `MessageFn` fields with the spec's `Default` impl, verbatim. `impl ComponentMessages`.
- **`apply_drag_position(ctx, target, x, y)`** (§1.7): `Area` → `sat=x.clamp`, `light=(1-y).clamp`, preserve hue/alpha; `Channel(ch)` → `min + x.clamp*(max-min)` via `with_channel`. Base on `*ctx.value.pending()` so controlled drags accumulate.
- **`change_end_effect()`**: `PendingEffect::new(Effect::ChangeEnd, ...)` invoking `props.on_change_end` with `*ctx.value.pending()`.
- **Machine::transition**: disabled guard returns `None` for all but `Focus`/`Blur`/`SyncValue`/`SetProps`. Arms per spec mapped to typed effects:
  - `Closed + (Open|Toggle)` → `to(Open)`, `open.set(true)`, effects `AttachClickOutside` + `DetectEyedropper`.
  - `Open + (Close|Toggle)` → `to(Closed)`, `open.set(false)`, effect `DetachClickOutside`.
  - `Open + CloseOnInteractOutside` (guard `close_on_interact_outside`) / `Open + CloseOnEscape` (guard `close_on_escape`) → close. `Dragging + CloseOnInteractOutside` → `None` (suppress during drag).
  - `Open + DragStart` (guard `!readonly`) → `to(Dragging{target})` + `apply_drag_position`.
  - `Dragging{target} + DragMove` (guard `!readonly`) → `context_only` apply. `Dragging + DragEnd` → `to(Open)` + `change_end_effect`.
  - `(any) + SetColor` (guard `!readonly`) → set value. `Open + SetChannel` (guard `!readonly`) → `with_channel`. `(any) + SetFormat` → set format.
  - `(any) + ChangeColorSpace` → set `color_space` + effect `AnnounceColorSpace` (closure uses `ctx.messages.color_space_switched(&format!("{new_space:?}"), &ctx.locale)`; the **announce call itself is adapter-side** — core just emits the named effect, no `use_platform_effects` in core).
  - `Open + ChannelIncrement/Decrement` (guard `!readonly`) → clamp to `channel_range`.
  - `Open + EyedropperRequest` (guard `eyedropper_supported && !readonly`) → effect `InvokeEyedropper`. `(any) + EyedropperResult(Some)` → set value; `None` → `None`.
  - `(any) + SetEyedropperSupported(b)` → set flag. `(any) + Focus/Blur` → set/clear `focused_part`.
  - `(any) + SyncValue(v)` → `sync_controlled`. `(any) + SetProps` → sync mutable context props from `props`.
- **on_props_changed / initial_effects**: as in Canonical conventions above.
- **Part** (§1.8 revised): `Root, Label, Control, Trigger, Content, Area, AreaThumb, ChannelSlider{channel}, ChannelSliderThumb{channel}, AlphaSlider, SwatchGroup, Swatch{index}, FormatSelect, ChannelInput{channel,index}, HexInput, EyeDropperTrigger, HiddenInput`. `#[scope="color-picker"]`. **`Swatch` carries only `index`** (not `color: ColorValue` — `ColorValue` is not `Eq`/`Hash`, which `ComponentPart` requires); the color resolves from `ctx.swatches[index]`, matching `color_swatch_picker::Part::Item`.
- **Api** (§1.8): all `*_attrs` methods (ids via `ctx.ids.part(...)`); computed `is_open/is_dragging/value/value_as_string/format/color_name/color_announcement`; imperative `open/close/set_value/set_format`; typed dispatch helpers (`on_trigger_click`, `on_content_keydown` for Escape, `on_area_pointer_down`, `on_channel_slider_keydown`, `on_swatch_click`, `on_eyedropper_click`). `swatch_attrs(index)` reads `ctx.swatches.get(index)`. `ConnectApi::part_attrs` exhaustive over `Part`. Use `HtmlAttr::Role` (there is no `AriaAttr::Role`).

## Tests (TDD — write failing tests first)

**Unit (in-module `#[cfg(test)]`)** covering every issue bullet + acceptance: trigger `aria-haspopup="dialog"`/`aria-expanded`/`aria-controls`/`aria-labelledby`; open/close/toggle + `data-ars-state`; area drag updates sat+light; channel-slider drag updates channel; `channels()` per color space (RGB vs HSL); alpha slider attrs gated by `show_alpha`; `SetFormat` changes `value_as_string` across hex/rgb/hsl/hsb; eyedropper gating (`SetEyedropperSupported` enables `EyedropperRequest`; trigger `hidden` when unsupported; `EyedropperResult(Some)` sets value); swatch selected/`data-ars-index`; content `role="dialog"`+`aria-labelledby`; hidden input `name`+hex `value`; disabled/readonly guards; channel increment/decrement clamping; `ChangeColorSpace` emits `AnnounceColorSpace` + updates `color_space`; `on_change_end` fires on `DragEnd` with pending value; controlled open+value sync via `set_props`; `Dragging` suppresses `CloseOnInteractOutside`; `initial_effects` when `default_open`; one exhaustive events/parts walk for llvm-cov.

content.content:
1	//! Popover non-modal anchored overlay machine.
2	//!
3	//! Owns the binary `Closed`/`Open` state, modal flag, dismissal policy,
4	//! semantic IDs, ARIA/data attribute output, the adapter-allocated z-index,
5	//! the runtime placement reported by the adapter, and the adapter-resolvable
6	//! named effect intents listed in `spec/components/overlay/popover.md` §1.6.
7	//!
8	//! The agnostic core never traverses the DOM, never resolves elements by ID,
9	//! never attaches document listeners, and never inspects real event targets —
10	//! those responsibilities belong to the framework adapter (`ars-leptos`,
11	//! `ars-dioxus`). Adapters obtain live element references via `NodeRef`
12	//! (Leptos) / `MountedData` (Dioxus); the ID fields in `Context` exist purely
13	//! for ARIA wiring and hydration-stable `id` attributes.
14	//!
15	//! Click-outside containment, focus restoration, portal host resolution, and
16	//! geometry measurement all stay in the adapter layer. The state machine
17	//! receives back the resulting placement/arrow/z-index data through the
18	//! [`Event::PositioningUpdate`] and [`Event::SetZIndex`] events.
19	//!
20	//! # Adapter mounting flow
21	//!
22	//! Adapter integrations (`ars-leptos::use_machine`, `ars-dioxus::use_machine`)
23	//! always follow the same first-mount sequence. The example below sketches
24	//! the flow against the agnostic [`Service`](ars_core::Service) directly so
25	//! the contract is visible without framework noise:
26	//!
27	//! ```
28	//! use ars_components::overlay::popover::{Effect, Event, Machine, Messages, Props};
29	//! use ars_core::{Env, Service};
30	//!
31	//! // 1. Construct the service. `default_open: true` boots straight into
32	//! //    `State::Open`; the regular open-plan transition does not run.
33	//! let mut service = Service::<Machine>::new(
34	//!     Props::new().id("popover-readme").default_open(true),
35	//!     &Env::default(),
36	//!     &Messages::default(),
37	//! );
38	//!
39	//! // 2. Drain the initial effects exactly once. The adapter would now
40	//! //    dispatch each named intent (allocate z-index, attach click-outside
41	//! //    listener, move focus into the content, fire `on_open_change`).
42	//! //    Adapter dispatch typically uses an exhaustive `match` on
43	//! //    `effect.name`; here we collect into the wire-string names so the
44	//! //    doctest is self-contained.
45	//! let initial: Vec<Effect> = service
46	//!     .take_initial_effects()
47	//!     .into_iter()
48	//!     .map(|effect| effect.name)
49	//!     .collect();
50	//!
51	//! assert!(initial.contains(&Effect::OpenChange));
52	//! assert!(initial.contains(&Effect::AllocateZIndex));
53	//! assert!(initial.contains(&Effect::AttachClickOutside));
54	//! assert!(initial.contains(&Effect::FocusInitial));
55	//!
56	//! // 3. Subsequent calls observe an empty buffer — the contract guarantees
57	//! //    each effect fires exactly once.
58	//! assert!(service.take_initial_effects().is_empty());
59	//!
60	//! // 4. From here on, regular event dispatch produces effects through
61	//! //    `SendResult::pending_effects` like any other component.
62	//! let close = service.send(Event::Close);
63	//! assert!(close.state_changed);
64	//! ```

86	/// Typed identifier for every named effect intent the popover machine

88	///
89	/// Adapters that dispatch on names write
90	/// `match effect.name { popover::Effect::OpenChange => …, … }`
91	/// exhaustively, so name typos and unhandled variants surface at compile
92	/// time — the same convention used by the dialog and tooltip machines.
93	/// The variant names themselves are the contract; there is no parallel
94	/// kebab-case wire form to keep in sync.
95	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
96	pub enum Effect {
97	    /// Emitted on every state-flipping transition (`Closed → Open` and
98	    /// `Open → Closed`) and on a non-Closed initial mount. The adapter
99	    /// resolves the intent by reading the post-transition open state from
100	    /// the connected [`Api`] (via [`Api::is_open`]) and invoking
101	    /// [`Props::on_open_change`] with that value.
102	    OpenChange,
103

105	    /// Adapters install a click-outside listener using the
106	    /// click-outside race-prevention strategy documented in spec §1.5
107	    /// (rAF deferral or timestamp comparison) and dispatch
108	    /// [`Event::CloseOnInteractOutside`] when an outside interaction is
109	    /// detected.
110	    AttachClickOutside,
111	
112	    /// Emitted on every `Open → Closed` transition. Adapters MUST remove
113	    /// the previously installed click-outside listener and cancel any
114	    /// pending rAF callback so a stale listener does not attach to an
115	    /// already-closed popover.
116	    DetachClickOutside,
117	
118	    /// Emitted on `Closed → Open` and on a non-Closed initial mount
119	    /// (see spec §1.6 and `spec/shared/z-index-stacking.md`). Adapters
120	    /// MUST allocate a z-index from the active

content.text: All 33 logic tests pass except one, and the 27 snapshot failures are just missing `.snap` files (expected). The real failure is `channel_increment_and_decrement_clamp_to_range`: incrementing hue to 360 wraps to 0 because `ColorValue` normalizes hue to `[0,360)`. I'll test clamping on a linear channel (Red) instead, which is the cleaner assertion.

content.content:
856	### 1.5 Guards
857	
858	```rust
859	/// Whether the component is disabled.
860	fn is_disabled(ctx: &Context, _props: &Props) -> bool {
861	    ctx.disabled
862	}
863	
864	/// Whether the component is read-only.
865	fn is_readonly(ctx: &Context, _props: &Props) -> bool {
866	    ctx.readonly
867	}
868	
869	/// Whether the component is open.
870	fn is_open(ctx: &Context, _props: &Props) -> bool {
871	    *ctx.open.get()
872	}
873	```
874	
875	### 1.6 Color Space Switching
876	
877	`ColorPicker` supports runtime color space switching via `Event::ChangeColorSpace(ColorSpace)`.
878	
879	**Transition Behavior** for `ChangeColorSpace(new_space)`:
880	
881	1. **Recompute value**: Convert the current color value from the old color space to `new_space`. Preserve perceptual magnitudes where possible (e.g., HSL->HSB preserves hue and saturation, converts lightness to brightness).
882	2. **Update context**: Set `ctx.color_space = new_space`. Update `ctx.value` with the converted color.
883	3. **Remap channels**: The color area and channel sliders update to reflect the new space's channels (e.g., switching from RGB to HSL changes the area from Red/Green to Hue/Saturation, and the slider from Blue to Lightness).
884	4. **Live region announcement**: Emit an `aria-live="polite"` announcement: `"Switched to {space} color space"` (e.g., "Switched to HSL color space"). This informs screen reader users that the control layout has changed.
885	
886	**UI Affordance**: The color space selector (button group, dropdown, or segmented control) emits `Event::ChangeColorSpace(selected_space)` when the user selects a different space. The spec does not prescribe a specific UI pattern -- adapters choose the appropriate control.
887	
888	**Supported Spaces**: All spaces in the `ColorSpace` enum (RGB, HSL, HSB/HSV, HEX) are switchable at runtime. The conversion between spaces is lossless for the sRGB gamut (minor floating-point rounding is acceptable).
889	
890	### 1.7 Full Machine Implementation
891

### 1.8 Connect / API

```rust
#[derive(ComponentPart)]
#[scope = "color-picker"]
pub enum Part {
    Root,
    Label,
    Control,
    Trigger,
    Content,
    Area,
    AreaThumb,
    /// A channel slider container, parameterized by channel.
    ChannelSlider { channel: ColorChannel },
    /// A channel slider thumb, parameterized by channel.
    ChannelSliderThumb { channel: ColorChannel },
    AlphaSlider,
    SwatchGroup,
    /// A preset swatch button. The color is resolved from `Context::swatches`
    /// at `index`; `ColorValue` is not `Eq`/`Hash` (it holds `f64`s), which
    /// `ComponentPart` requires, so the variant carries only the index — the
    /// same pattern as `color_swatch_picker::Part::Item`.
    Swatch { index: usize },
    FormatSelect,
    /// A channel text input, parameterized by channel and display index.
    ChannelInput { channel: ColorChannel, index: usize },
    HexInput,
    EyeDropperTrigger,
    HiddenInput,

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-300/.claude/skills/post-implementation-audit

# Post-implementation audit

This skill is the bridge between *"the named tests pass"* and *"the user reviews the diff"*. It exists because the initial implementation of any task is usually correct on its surface acceptance criteria but quietly drifts from the spec, leaves untested defensive code, ships APIs that diverge from convention, or carries a fold-vs-lowercase mistake that won't surface until production. Running these audits before user-review collapses three reviewer round-trips into one.

The skill is mandatory per CLAUDE.md "Development Workflow" step 7 — run it after implementing any user-requested task (feature, bug fix, refactor) and before presenting the final result for user review.

## Quick flow

The audit is strictly linear — no branching, just a loop in Phase 2:

```
Phase 1 (spec/impl drift audit)
   └─→ land all Phase 1 findings
        └─→ Phase 2 (iterative "anything missing?")
              ├─→ Round 1: surface findings → land all
              ├─→ Round 2: surface findings → land all
              └─→ keep looping until 2 consecutive clean rounds (minimum 2 rounds total)
                   └─→ Phase 3 (test coverage)
                        └─→ land all Phase 3 findings
                             └─→ Final verification suite
                                  └─→ Hand off to user (CLAUDE.md step 8)
```

The body below walks through each phase in order. Read top-to-bottom; the structure mirrors the execution order.

## Operating principle: no deferral

**Every recommendation each audit phase produces lands in *this* PR.** Don't open follow-up issues. Don't TODO-flag. Don't promise a future PR or a future sprint. Don't quietly skip a finding because it feels like scope creep.

If a finding is genuinely out of scope (e.g., requires a workspace-wide refactor touching 50+ files), surface it explicitly to the user with that reasoning visible — but the default is to land everything. This constraint is what makes the audit valuable: a deferred finding is a guarantee that the original implementation's bias survives into `main`, where it costs ten times as much to undo.

The user will tell you "land it all". Anticipate that, and just do it.

## Three sequential phases

Run the phases **in order**. Each phase fully completes (audit → land all findings → re-verify) before the next begins.

---

### Phase 1 — spec/implementation drift audit

Compare the implementation against the authoritative spec (`spec/components/<category>/<component>.md` plus any foundation specs it transitively depends on) section-by-section. For every drift, propose the **best outcome independent of which side is currently right**. Sometimes the spec wins, sometimes the impl wins, sometimes the right answer is neither.

The canonical entry-prompt for this phase (use this wording when the user asks you to start the audit explicitly):

> "Now let's do an audit on how well our new code follows our spec. Do an in-depth check for spec drift and suggest recommendations for each finding. In those recommendations, disregard both the spec and the current implementation as defaults — the recommendation should be the best outcome possible. Sometimes that's the spec, sometimes the implementation, sometimes a mix, sometimes a totally different thing."

#### Precondition: both files must exist

Before reading anything, verify the touched implementation has a corresponding spec file at `spec/components/<category>/<name>.md` (or the relevant `spec/foundation/` path for foundation-layer changes). If the spec is missing, **stop the audit and surface this to the user** — an unspecced module is a planning failure that should be resolved (write the spec, or split the change) before auditing, not papered over by this skill. Likewise, if the implementation file referenced by the issue is missing or empty, surface that as a precondition failure rather than auditing against an empty diff.

#### Procedure

1. Read the spec file end-to-end (use `Read` with no offset/limit so you have the whole thing).
2. Read the implementation file end-to-end (same).
3. Walk every public surface declared in the spec — every struct, enum, function, method, derive list, default value, constant, public re-export. For each:
   - Does the spec define it? Does the impl match exactly?
   - Are there extras in the impl not in the spec? Are they justified?
   - Are there spec items the impl skipped? Are they critical?
   - **Does the spec's prose match the impl's actual behavior?** This is the silent-contract-violation class — words that mean something specific in the spec but were implemented as something close-but-different (e.g., "case folding" in the spec but `to_lowercase` in the impl).
4. For each finding, write up:
   - What the spec says, with line numbers
   - What the impl does, with line numbers
   - Why it drifted (often a tradeoff the implementer made under acceptance-criteria pressure)
   - **Best-outcome recommendation** — independent of which side is currently right
5. Group findings by severity (High / Medium / Low) and present as a table to the user.
6. Land all of them. Update the spec where the impl is right, update the impl where the spec is right, do both where neither was right, change *only* the spec wording (and add tests proving the new wording) when the contract was subtly wrong all along.

#### Special case: both the spec and the implementation are wrong

A small fraction of drift findings are *not* "one side is right, the other drifted" — both sides are incorrect, incomplete, or built on a flawed premise. This is rare but real (the task #204 eszett case is an example: the spec implied `ß ↔ ss` equivalence, the impl used `to_lowercase`, but neither matched the actual TR21 case-folding semantics needed). Handle this case differently from routine drift:

1. **Escalate to the user via `AskUserQuestion`** — don't unilaterally pick a "best outcome" when no precedent exists. A both-wrong case is a real design decision, not a routine drift fix; the user owns the call.
2. **Once the user picks a direction**, land *all three* together in this same PR: the new spec wording, the new implementation, and the tests proving the new design. Skipping any of the three leaves a different kind of drift behind.
3. **Write the test first** (TDD per CLAUDE.md), watch it fail under the old code, then change the impl, then update the spec to describe what the new tests assert.

#### Output table format

| Finding ID | Severity | What spec says | What impl does | Best outcome | Where to land |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| H1 | High | … | … | … | spec §X / impl `path:line` |

End with a disposition summary: *"N spec-only changes, M impl-only changes, K both, Q status-quo-was-correct."*

---

### Phase 2 — iterative "anything else missing?" passes

Once Phase 1 findings are landed, the next blind spot is the surface Phase 1 didn't look at: adapter specs, feature flags, prelude exports, framework wiring, foundation patterns that deserve promotion, catalog/manifest entries. This phase is **iterative** — keep asking "anything else?" until two consecutive rounds find nothing new.

The canonical entry-prompt (one per round):

> "Any other improvement that we might still be missing?"

#### Surfaces to actively check each round

This is a non-exhaustive checklist. Each round, walk through it explicitly:

- **Adapter specs** at `spec/leptos-components/<category>/<component>.md` and `spec/dioxus-components/<category>/<component>.md` — do they describe the new core surface accurately? Are their canonical implementation sketches up-to-date with the actual `Api` / function signatures? Do they reference the right helper methods (e.g., `Api::root_attrs()`) instead of hardcoded data attribute strings?
- **Adapter feature wiring** in `crates/ars-leptos/Cargo.toml` and `crates/ars-dioxus/Cargo.toml` — does the adapter's `icu4x` / `web-intl` feature need to enable any new `ars-components/<feature>` flag for the new code to be reachable?
- **Adapter `prelude.rs`** — does the new public API need to flow through? (Only if an adapter wrapper exists for the new component; if not, defer to the adapter task.)
- **Workspace `spec/manifest.toml`** and **`foundation/02-component-catalog.md`** — is the new component registered and statused? (For brand-new components only.)
- **Foundation specs** (`spec/foundation/00-*` through `spec/foundation/11-*`) — does the new code expose a missing shared abstraction worth promoting? CLAUDE.md `spec/CLAUDE.md` says: *"If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec."*
- **New crate dependencies** — was the user notified per CLAUDE.md's "Do not add a new dependency crate without explicit user approval first" rule? Did the implementation add `i18n`-style feature flags that warrant a CLAUDE.md note?
- **`.cargo/mutants.toml`** — are any new equivalent mutations documented with justifications? (Phase 3 will validate this; surface it here if a mutation that *should* be equivalent isn't documented yet.)

#### Termination

Stop when:

- Two consecutive rounds surface no new findings, **OR**
- Three rounds have been completed and the remaining findings are honestly out-of-scope (genuine workspace-wide refactors that would derail this PR).

Do **not** stop after one clean round — the second round nearly always surfaces items the first ignored because they felt out-of-scope. With the "no deferral" rule active, they're back in scope.

#### Output per round

A list of findings with the same "best outcome" framing as Phase 1, plus an explicit statement at the end: *"That's everything I can find this round. Want me to do another?"* — knowing the answer will be yes for at least one more round.

---

### Phase 3 — test coverage audit

By this point the code is convention-aligned and well-specced. Now check the **test surface** — both depth (line / branch / mutation coverage) and breadth (does every test type that *should* exist for this kind of change exist?).

The canonical entry-prompt:

> "How good is our test coverage here, by looking at the code paths, checking the coverage report and considering the many kinds of tests that we are intending to have (including snapshot tests, proptests, and e2e tests)? Can it be improved?"

#### Test types and what each catches

| Test type | What it catches | How to assess for the new code |
|---|---|---|
| **Unit tests** (inline `#[cfg(test)]`) | Logic + edge cases on the named acceptance criteria | List every public API + every edge case the spec describes; cross-reference with the test list |
| **Snapshot tests** (insta) | `AttrMap` / chunk-output shape regressions | Check coverage of every anatomy part and every output-affecting prop / state / context branch |
| **Property tests** (proptest) | Invariant violations across the input space | Check that invariants are non-trivial (roundtrip, idempotence, no-adjacent-X, etc.) and the input strategy is broad enough to actually exercise edge locales / multi-byte / large inputs |
| **Mutation tests** (`cargo xmutants`) | Tests that *exist* but don't actually constrain behavior | **Not run as part of this audit.** Owned by the Nightly CI `mutation-testing` job; `MISSED` mutants are triaged in a follow-up, never as a pre-PR gate. Use the coverage report + breadth audit to judge test quality here. |
| **Doc tests** | Public-API examples broken by future changes | Check `///` blocks for at least one `# Examples` block on the canonical entry point |
| **Spec-conformance tests** | Anatomy drift between spec and impl | Check `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/*.rs` for an entry covering the new component's `Part` enum and public API/attribute contract; this is mandatory for every new framework-agnostic component |
| **Code coverage** (cargo llvm-cov + xtask wasm path) | Unreachable / under-tested code; threshold drift | Use the right command for the crate (see "Procedure" below) — bare `cargo llvm-cov` does **not** measure adapter wasm code paths. Always finish with `cargo xtask coverage check-all --file <lcov>` to enforce the same thresholds CI does. |
| **E2E / browser tests** (wasm-bindgen-test) | DOM-level behavior in the adapter | N/A for agnostic-core changes. Required when the change touches `ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`, `ars-dom`, or `ars-i18n` (`web-intl` path); these run via `cargo xtask test` or the adapter test-harness crates and execute in a browser via `wasm-bindgen-test`. Treat coverage from the wasm path (`cargo xtask coverage wasm`) as the source of truth for these — not the host-target llvm-cov number. |

#### Procedure

1. **Coverage report.** The command depends on which crate the change touches — this workspace measures host-target and wasm32 paths separately because adapter code (`ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`, `ars-dom`, the `ars-i18n` `web-intl` path, the adapter test harnesses) has `target_arch = "wasm32"` / `feature = "web"` code that bare host-target `cargo llvm-cov` cannot reach.

   **For native-only crates** (`ars-components`, `ars-collections`, `ars-core`, `ars-forms`, `ars-interactions`, `ars-a11y`, `xtask`):
   ```bash
   cargo llvm-cov test -p <crate> --features <relevant> --lib --no-fail-fast --text -- <module>::
   ```

   **For adapter crates / `web-intl` paths** (`ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`, `ars-dom`, `ars-test-harness-leptos`, `ars-test-harness-dioxus`, or `ars-i18n` when the change is in the `web-intl` feature gate):
   ```bash
   cargo xtask coverage wasm \
     --package <crate> \
     --feature <feat1> --feature <feat2> \
     --file wasm.lcov.info
   ```
   (`--feature` is singular and repeatable, NOT `--features` — it mirrors cargo's `--feature` convention; use `--no-default-features` to drop defaults.) This uses `wasm-bindgen-test`'s experimental coverage recipe with LLVM 22 / `clang-22` (the same recipe CI nightly runs via `cargo xtask ci coverage`). The bare `cargo llvm-cov` command will silently report `0.00%` for the wasm-gated code paths, which is *not* the same as having no coverage — it means *unmeasured*. Don't trust low numbers on adapter crates from the native path.

   **For a change that touches both native and wasm paths** (e.g., a new ars-i18n helper consumed from both backends, or a refactor in `ars-dom` that affects native callers):
   ```bash
   # Generate the native lcov (workspace, excluding wasm-only crates):
   cargo llvm-cov --workspace \
     --exclude ars-leptos --exclude ars-dioxus \
     --exclude ars-test-harness-leptos --exclude ars-test-harness-dioxus \
     --exclude ars-derive --exclude xtask \
     --lcov --output-path native.lcov.info

   # Generate the wasm lcov for each adapter target you touched:
   cargo xtask coverage wasm --package <wasm-package> --feature <feat> --file wasm.lcov.info

   # Merge without double-counting duplicate lines (each `--file` adds an input):
   cargo xtask coverage merge \
     --file native.lcov.info \
     --file wasm.lcov.info \
     --output merged.lcov.info

   # Enforce per-crate spec-defined thresholds (same gate CI runs):
   cargo xtask coverage check-all --file merged.lcov.info
   ```

   The per-crate thresholds (line% / branch%) are encoded in `xtask::coverage::default_thresholds()` and include the wasm targets. `cargo xtask coverage check-all` is the same gate CI enforces, so a green local run guarantees green CI on the coverage step.

   Read the per-line annotated output from the `--text` invocations. Every `^0` marker is an unhit branch — investigate each one. Categorize as: real test gap / equivalent defensive guard / unreachable dead code. **Expected non-gaps:** no-op callback closures in tests (e.g., `|_| {}`) are intentionally uncovered and not a real gap — CLAUDE.md calls this out explicitly.

   **Not measured by either path:** `ars-derive` (proc-macro, runs in the compiler — exercised indirectly via `crates/ars-core/tests/derive_contract.rs`). Don't expect coverage numbers here.

2. **Mutation testing — NOT a gate here.** Do **not** run `cargo xmutants` as part of this audit, and do not block handoff on a clean local mutation run. Mutation coverage is owned by the Nightly CI `mutation-testing` job; surviving (`MISSED`) mutants it reports are triaged in a dedicated follow-up (add a killing test, or add a justified `.cargo/mutants.toml` `exclude_re` for a true equivalent mutation), not during initial delivery. Lean on the coverage report (step 1) and the breadth audit (step 3) to judge test quality instead. (A voluntary local mutation pass is allowed but never required.)

3. **Test-type breadth audit.** Walk the table above. For each test type, ask: *"Does this kind of change need this kind of test, and do we have one?"* Examples:
   - New framework-agnostic component with an anatomy table → spec-conformance test required
   - New public function → doc test with `# Examples` required
   - New invariants on a computation pipeline → proptest invariants strongly recommended
   - New `AttrMap` helpers → snapshot tests for each branch required

4. **Cumulative assessment.** Present the before/after metrics in a table:

| Metric | Before this phase | After this phase |
|---|---|---|
| Lines covered | x% | y% |
| Mutations caught | x/total | y/total |
| Unit tests | N | M |
| Doc tests | N | M |
| Spec-conformance test | yes/no | yes/no |

#### Output

A list of gaps with concrete reproducers (specific test inputs that trigger the missed branch). For each gap, classify the disposition (real-gap-add-test / equivalent-mutation-skip / dead-code-remove). End with a clear *"land all"* and the verification commands to confirm.

#### Anti-pattern: chasing 100% line coverage

The goal is **not** "100% line coverage at any cost". The goal is *"strong, behavior-constraining tests with no unreachable defensive code, and every test type that should exist does exist"*. A 99% line-coverage report whose tests make positive-direction assertions is stronger than a 100% report full of assertion-free walks. If the last 1% of uncovered code is a genuinely unreachable defensive guard, leave it. (Mutation testing — the eventual measure of whether tests *constrain* behavior — is owned by Nightly CI, not this audit.)

---

## After the three phases

Run the full verification suite one final time before handing off to the user:

```bash
# 1. Unit + integration tests, both feature-on and feature-off variants
cargo test -p <crate> --features <relevant> --all-targets
cargo test -p <crate> --lib                       # without the optional features

# 2. Workspace clippy (must be -D warnings clean)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --exclude ars-i18n -- -D warnings

# 3. Backend matrices for any feature-gated code
cargo clippy -p ars-i18n --no-default-features --features std,icu4x --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo clippy -p ars-i18n --no-default-features --features std,web-intl --all-targets -- -D warnings

# 4. Docs build (0 new warnings on the changed crate)
cargo doc -p <crate> --no-deps --all-features

# 5. Spec validation + snippet check
cargo xtask spec validate
cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets

# 6. Snapshot orphan check
cargo insta test --workspace --features <relevant> --unreferenced=reject

# 7. (Mutation testing is NOT run here — it is Nightly-CI-driven.)

# 8. Coverage thresholds — uses the same gate CI enforces. The recipe
#    depends on which crate the change touches: native-only changes can
#    use `cargo llvm-cov ... --lcov`; changes that touch adapter code or
#    `web-intl` paths require `cargo xtask coverage wasm` + `merge` first
#    (see Phase 3 above). Then:
cargo xtask coverage check-all --file lcov.info

# 9. Adapter builds reachable with feature wiring
cargo build -p ars-leptos --features icu4x
cargo build -p ars-dioxus --features icu4x,web
```

Then write **one consolidated user summary** covering all three phases' findings + the verification table. Hand off for user review per CLAUDE.md workflow step 8 (which was the old step 7 before this skill was inserted).

After the user approves commit/push and the PR is opened, **read and follow**
`.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` through to Codex 👍. That
skill is AGENTS.md step 12 — posting `@codex review` is only the trigger inside
the loop, not a substitute for the full poll/triage/fix cycle.

## Anti-patterns

- **Deferring** any finding ("we can fix that in a follow-up"). This is the rule the user set explicitly. Breaking it makes the audit ceremonial.
- **Loading the user with options mid-phase.** Make a clear best-outcome recommendation. Only escalate to `AskUserQuestion` when there's a genuine judgment call the user must make (e.g., behavior choices with real tradeoffs, not implementation details).
- **Skipping rounds of Phase 2** because the first round was clean. The second round nearly always finds something the first ignored.
- **Treating coverage % as the goal.** The goal is behavior-constraining tests and breadth-complete coverage, not a number.
- **Inventing cosmetic edits** the audits didn't surface. If a phase doesn't find a real finding, don't fabricate one — say "this round found nothing" and proceed.
- **Re-running tests after every micro-edit** during a phase. Land a batch of related findings, then verify, then move on.
- **Forgetting to update the named-tests file** when a new test exposes a real bug fixed in the same phase. The fix and the test land together.

## What this skill is not

- A code-review skill. Code review (style, naming, simplification) belongs to `pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer` and runs after the PR is opened.
- A pre-implementation skill. Before implementing, `feature-dev:feature-dev` and `superpowers:writing-plans` handle planning.
- A way to revisit acceptance criteria. The acceptance criteria are fixed by the GitHub issue. This skill assumes they're already met.

## Worked example — task #204

The flow this skill encodes was validated on GitHub issue #204 (`task: Implement Highlight agnostic core`). The initial implementation passed all 8 named acceptance-criterion tests on first try. Then:

- **Phase 1** surfaced 16 drift findings, including a silent contract violation (the spec said *"Unicode case folding"* but the impl used `to_lowercase` — they aren't equivalent: under `to_lowercase`, query `"ss"` does not match text `"ß"`, violating the spec's explicit eszett contract). Fix: add `ars_i18n::case_fold`, switch the matching pipeline, rewrite the German test to assert the actual three-direction equivalence.
- **Phase 2 round 1** surfaced 4 more items (adapter spec alignment, adapter feature wiring, roundtrip invariant test, proptest invariants).
- **Phase 2 round 2** surfaced 3 more (a `case_fold` vs `to_lowercase` semantic gap, foundation/10 parametric-anatomy concept, `cargo xmutants` scout).
- **Phase 3** surfaced 4 coverage gaps (dead `continue` branch, `merge_ranges` else branch, fold-expansion partial-match defense, spec-conformance test). Pushed line coverage from 98.67% to 99.91% with all 57 mutations caught.

The cumulative effect: PR landed with one user review pass instead of three, and the spec contract (`ß ↔ ss`) is actually true instead of cosmetically true. That's the value this skill creates.
Starting **Phase 1 — spec/implementation drift audit**. I authored both sides, so I'll check honestly for the mismatches my rewrite may have introduced. Let me locate the specific drift points.

content.text: Let me reformat the table with a script so every row's pipes align to the widened column.

input.command:
cd /Users/ericson/Workspace/Rust/ars-ui.worktrees/impl-300
python3 - <<'PY'
path="spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md"
lines=open(path).read().split("\n")
# table spans lines index 1443..1461 (0-based 1443 is "| Part") -> find header
start=next(i for i,l in enumerate(lines) if l.startswith("| Part "))
end=next(i for i,l in enumerate(lines[start:], start) if l.strip()=="" )  # blank after table
rows=[]
for l in lines[start:end]:
    if set(l.strip())<=set("|-: "):  # separator row
        rows.append(None)
        continue
    cells=[c.strip() for c in l.strip().strip("|").split("|")]
    rows.append(cells)
ncol=len(next(r for r in rows if r))
widths=[0]*ncol
for r in rows:
    if r:
        for i,c in enumerate(r):
            widths[i]=max(widths[i],len(c))
def fmt(r):
    return "| "+" | ".join(c.ljust(widths[i]) for i,c in enumerate(r))+" |"
def sep():
    return "| "+" | ".join("-"*widths[i] for i in range(ncol))+" |"
out=[]
for r in rows:
    out.append(sep() if r is None else fmt(r))
lines[start:end]=out
open(path,"w").write("\n".join(lines))
print("realigned table rows", start+1, "to", end)
PY
